page: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server belonging to the EDSv4 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, featuring 10 physical cores and 20 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server is equipped with 768 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results place the E20ds_v4 consistently in the average tier across most compute and memory operations. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1271.0 and a multi-core score of 8298.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 19266.4. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 1038.89 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 41.59 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by a Redis SET performance of 17.23 million operations per second and a Passmark database score of 7262.76.

While the server handles standard data processing well, it exhibits performance limitations in specific workloads. LLM inference benchmarks show weak performance for 135M model text generation at 71.75 tokens per second and 7B model prompt processing at 30.52 tokens per second. However, its high memory capacity and local SSD storage make it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not require GPU acceleration. The E20ds_v4 is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: The Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 20 vCPUs operating at 2.6 GHz, paired with 160.0 GB of RAM and 768 GB of local storage. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a Passmark CPU score of 19266.4 and a Redis SET throughput of 17.23 million operations per second. However, the server exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation and prompt processing. With its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU and local SSD capacity, the E20ds_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for databases, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB local storage, delivering average database and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 20 vCPUs and 160 GB RAM
768 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in database operations and memory bandwidth
Weak performance in LLM text generation and prompt processing
Suited for in-memory caching, databases, and memory-intensive applications
